description: The deepest aspect of personal identity; the continuous thread of self through time. 'Norea' names what persists—the who beneath all doing and having.
sound_symbolism: The word settles deep. 'No' grounds with internal presence, self found. 'Re' resonates with continuing thread, identity through time. Then 'a' opens to acceptance of this mystery.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'mia norea' (my soul). The deepest self. Related to but distinct from 'phiora' (spirit) which animates, while 'norea' identifies.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Soul as process—not permanent essence but the continuity of pattern, like a river's identity through flowing water.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Soul as sacred center—the part of person that connects to something greater.
